question.text: Regarding Top-up and Super Top-up health insurance policies, which of the following statements is correct?

answer.text: They do not have any deductible and cover all expenses from the first rupee
They are designed to replace the base health insurance policy entirely
They are designed with a fixed deductible and provide coverage above that threshold, supplementing the base policy
They can only be purchased from the same insurer who provides the base health policy

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Top-up and Super Top-up health insurance policies are specifically designed with a fixed deductible. They provide coverage for expenses that exceed the deductible amount. The deductible is typically set equal to the base policy's sum insured, so the top-up/super top-up kicks in when the base policy is exhausted. **The Trap:** Option A is incorrect because deductibles are the defining feature of top-up policies. Option D is incorrect because these policies can be purchased from any insurer, not necessarily the same one that provides the base cover. **Cross-Exam Context:** Top-up and Super Top-up policies are tested in NISM Series X-B. The difference between a Top-up (deductible per claim) and Super Top-up (deductible on aggregate claims in a year) is an advanced concept. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Super Top-up policies are more client-friendly than plain Top-ups because the deductible is applied on aggregate claims in a policy year, not per hospitalisation. They are a cost-effective way to increase health coverage without paying for a high base sum insured.
